anti_claims: This paper-module JSON row does not prove runtime correctness, release readiness, or whole-system completeness.
Legacy Markdown presence is not treated as source authority or as typed subject evidence.
Capsule-backed subject/code edges are structural references only; they do not prove implementation correctness.

compression.authority_ceiling: Command-receipt evidence over bounded public fixtures, not runtime-product completeness. A pass means the curated route-packet cases recomputed as expected (positives accepted, negatives rejected by recomputation with the expected markers); it does NOT establish live private-kernel navigation quality, embedding quality, universal benchmark authority, production readiness, or release/publication authorization. Receipts are body-free command evidence, not proof of substrate correctness.
compression.card: Navigation Fitness Benchmark surfaces the engine-room route-packet benchmark capsule. Each fixture pairs a navigation task (expected stable ids, forbidden first routes, latency budget, scent terms) with the route packet a router actually produced. The capsule recomputes recall/precision against the expected ids, checks the first route against the forbidden list, scores scent coverage, derives a latency verdict, and collects sufficiency/latency debt — then reports whether the recomputation matches the case's planted expectation. Two positive cases match their expectations and are accepted (one carrying an honestly-anticipated latency debt); two negative cases plant a route defect plus a deliberately-wrong "it passed" expectation, which the recomputation contradicts, so they are rejected with the real failure marker (missing_id, forbidden_route) firing. Scope: curated public fixtures only — not a live kernel run, not an embedding benchmark, not a universal navigation score, not release authority.
compression.one_line: Recomputes a curated route-packet navigation benchmark — recall, precision, forbidden-first-route, scent coverage, latency, and debt — over bounded public fixtures, accepting a case only when the recomputation matches its planted expectation.

omitted: public Markdown prose body round-trip equivalence
legacy-only subject edges not named by JSON capsules
omission_receipt.reason: Paper-module instance preserves capsule fields or a legacy Markdown path inventory. Legacy-only rows do not flip source authority; required subjects and selective law/neighbour/code edges remain residual unless source JSON names ids.

residual_pressure.reentry_condition: Author JSON capsules and typed subject/edge rows from source evidence, then regenerate paper-module JSON instances and projections.

paper_module_payload.authority_ceiling: Command-receipt evidence over bounded public fixtures, not runtime-product completeness. A pass means the curated route-packet cases recomputed as expected (positives accepted, negatives rejected by recomputation with the expected markers); it does NOT establish live private-kernel navigation quality, embedding quality, universal benchmark authority, production readiness, or release/publication authorization. Receipts are body-free command evidence, not proof of substrate correctness.

code_loci.role: Organ wrapper that surfaces the capsule under the standard organ contract: bounded fixture cases, two planted positive and two planted negative cases, an aggregate pass/fail build_result, a body-free result_card, body-free validation/acceptance receipts via microcosm_core.receipts, and CLI dispatch. _evaluate_case calls the REAL capsule evaluate_case (no re-implementation, no baked answers) and decides observed_ok from expectation_met plus, for negatives, the presence of the expected recomputed failure marker.

Public-safe, source-faithful refactor of system/lib/navigation_fitness.py. Given route-packet fixtures, it recomputes per-task recall and precision of selected artifacts against expected stable ids (with prefix-glob matching), checks the first-contact command against a forbidden-first-route list, scores scent-term coverage, derives a latency verdict against a per-task budget, classifies a sufficiency status with a typed failure kind, and aggregates summary metrics (p50/p95 wall, pass/fail counts) plus sufficiency and latency debt candidates. evaluate_case also re-derives the benchmark and reports whether the recomputation matches a case's planted expectation.
symbols: Curated route-packet benchmark evaluator for expected stable ids
forbidden_hits = [route for route in task.forbidden_first_routes if route and route in first_contact_command]
